title: Odoo Supply-Chain End-To-End Workflow
instruction:
## Goal

Complete the recovered end-to-end Odoo supply-chain workflow and leave the final business state inside the task database.

## Variant

Four variants share the same workflow but differ in active database and run-tag namespace: `odoo_compact`, `odoo_shifted`, `odoo_stress`, `odoo_hard`. Work only inside the variant's own database (e.g. `odoo_compact`) and stamp the matching run tag (e.g. `odoo_compact`) into searchable fields such as Reference / Notes / Customer Ref / Vendor Ref on documents you create.

## Workflow (summary — see `input/prompt.txt` for exact codes and quantities)

1. **Global config.** USD company currency; enable EUR and CNY with locked FX: `1 EUR = 1.20 USD`, `1 CNY = 0.125 USD`. Stockable inventory valuation: FIFO + Automated. Warehouse `WH` uses 2-step receipts; warehouse `My Co` uses 3-step delivery.
2. **Master data.** Customer `CUST-EU01`; vendors `V-CN01`, `V-US01`, `V-US02`, `V-SUB01`, `V-DS01`. Products `FP-1000`, `SA-200`, `SC-300`, `RM-010`, `RM-020`, `RM-030`, `RM-040`, `ACC-900`, and service `FREIGHT-100`. Define BoMs for `FP-1000`, `SA-200`, and the `SC-300` subcontract.
3. **Sales.** Create an EUR pricelist and a sales order for `FP-1000 x3` and `ACC-900 x3` (with the variant tag), triggering MTO → Manufacture for `FP-1000` and Dropship for `ACC-900`.
4. **Purchases.** Issue tagged POs in CNY / USD for the raw materials, the subcontract fee, and the dropship PO.
5. **Landed cost.** Receive the CNY PO into `WH` using 2-step receipts, create one Landed Cost (`FREIGHT-100 = 100 USD`, split *By Current Cost*), validate it, complete the input-to-stock transfer, and move `RM-010` / `RM-020` onto the `My Co` warehouse.
6. **Subcontracting.** Resupply the subcontractor with `RM-040 x3`, then receive `SC-300 x3` with serials `CS-0001..CS-0003`.
7. **Manufacturing with shortage recovery.** Produce `SA-200 x3`; scrap `RM-030 x1` mid-run to force a shortage, raise an extra tagged PO to replace it, then finish `SA-200 x3` and `FP-1000 x3` with serials `SSK-0001..SSK-0003`.
8. **Delivery.** Ship `FP-1000` from `My Co` in two waves (2 + 1) using 3-step delivery; `ACC-900` is delivered via Dropship directly from vendor to customer.
9. **Invoicing & payments.** After each delivery, post EUR invoices totalling `460 EUR` and `230 EUR` and register payments to *Paid*.
10. **Vendor bills.** Post and reconcile vendor bills for every PO plus the freight bill — at least six paid vendor bills.
11. **Expenses.** Record one analytic expense line so the account-analytic evidence fires for the run tag.
12. **Return + credit note.** Return serial `SSK-0002` from the customer back into `My Co` stock and post and settle a `200 EUR` credit note.

## Evidence to save in `output/`

- `lc_split.png` — landed-cost allocation screen showing `RM-010 = 60.00` / `RM-020 = 40.00`
- `invoices.png` — paid customer invoices totalling `460 EUR` and `230 EUR`
- `return_credit.png` — `SSK-0002` return plus the `200 EUR` credit note
- `stock_wh.png` — warehouse balances showing `WH: RM-010=0, RM-020=0` and `My Co: RM-010=4, RM-020=7`
- `submission.txt` — short operator note confirming the variant DB / tag and any caveats

## Environment pointers

- Local Odoo web server: `http://localhost:8069/web`
- Launcher: `software/launch_odoo.cmd` (opens the correct DB in the default browser)
- Task setup resets the selected variant database from a clean template using the public setup tools.

input_materials.name: Agent-visible workflow prompt
input_materials.description: Full recovered workflow contract for the active variant — master data codes, FX rates, warehouse routes, tagged purchases, subcontracting, shortage recovery, invoicing, expenses, and the mandatory return / credit-note flow.

input_materials_summary: Agent-visible workflow prompt [`.txt`] @ input/prompt.txt: Full recovered workflow contract for the active variant — master data codes, FX rates, warehouse routes, tagged purchases, subcontracting, shortage recovery, invoicing, expenses, and the mandatory return / credit-note flow.

release_scope_note: Trial subset includes task instructions, input-material manifests, software metadata, and any available local agent-visible input files. It excludes hidden gold outputs and evaluator gold files.

title: Humanoid Velocity Tracking Policy
instruction:
You are producing a policy submission for an Isaac Lab humanoid velocity-tracking benchmark.

## Variant
`base`: Humanoid velocity tracking policy

## Input Files
- Task brief: `input/task_brief.md`
- Success criteria and exact evaluation command: `input/SUCCESS.md`
- Observation/action ordering: `input/PROPRIO_SPEC.md`
- Robot asset and articulation config: `input/assets/`
- Evaluation harness: `input/eval/`
- Starter template: `input/submission_template/`

## What You Need To Do
1. Read the task brief, proprioceptive-state spec, and success criteria.
2. Train, distill, hand-author, or otherwise produce a deterministic policy that implements the required `Policy` API.
3. Save exactly two files in `output/`: `policy.py` and `checkpoint.pt`.

## Output Requirements
- `policy.py` defines `Policy(checkpoint_path: str, device: str)`.
- `Policy.inference(obs)` returns exactly `{"action": tensor}`.
- The action tensor has shape `(N, 23)`, dtype `torch.float32`, and lives on the same device as `obs["command"]`.
- Do not write final answers outside `output/`.

input_materials.name: task_brief.md
input_materials.description: Agent-facing summary of the policy submission task.

input_materials.name: SUCCESS.md
input_materials.description: Hard gates, reference evaluation command, metrics, and acceptance thresholds.

input_materials.name: PROPRIO_SPEC.md
input_materials.description: Fixed observation vector and 23-DOF action ordering.

input_materials.name: assets/
input_materials.description: Humanoid URDF, meshes, and articulation configuration.

input_materials.name: eval/
input_materials.description: Isaac Lab evaluation environment and `run_eval.py` entry point.

title: ADAPT-VQE Molecular Ground-State Energy
instruction:
You are solving a molecular ground-state energy benchmark using only NumPy and SciPy on Ubuntu.

Read `input/problem_spec.md`, then use the visible Hamiltonians in `input/h2_hamiltonian.json`, `input/lih_hamiltonian.json`, and `input/beh2_hamiltonian.json`. Set up the runtime with `uv sync --frozen --project input/runtime_env`.

Implement dense-matrix VQE / ADAPT-VQE logic using only NumPy and SciPy, and write exactly one file, `output/results.json`, following the schema in the prompt.

input_materials.name: Problem specification
input_materials.description: Sanitized visible task statement and output schema.

input_materials.name: Tier Hamiltonians
input_materials.description: Visible Jordan-Wigner Hamiltonians for H2, LiH, and BeH2.

input_materials.name: Runtime manifest
input_materials.description: Pinned NumPy / SciPy runtime manifest for the agent.

input_materials_summary: Problem specification [Markdown] @ input/problem_spec.md: Sanitized visible task statement and output schema. | Tier Hamiltonians [JSON] @ input/{h2_hamiltonian.json,lih_hamiltonian.json,beh2_hamiltonian.json}: Visible Jordan-Wigner Hamiltonians for H2, LiH, and BeH2. | Runtime manifest [uv project] @ input/runtime_env/: Pinned NumPy / SciPy runtime manifest for the agent.

title: chroma_key_from_reference
instruction:
Goal: Remove the green-screen background from the source clip, create a new Resolve project yourself, composite the correct foreground subject into the intended target scene indicated by the first-frame target screenshot, and export the finished composite video.

Software:
- Launch DaVinci Resolve using `software/DaVinci Resolve.lnk`.
- No starter `.drp` project is packaged with this task. Create a new Resolve project yourself for the task run.

Inputs:
- Foreground source clip with green background: `input/input.mp4`
- First-frame target screenshot / disambiguation hint: `input/input.png`

What to do:
- Create a new Resolve project and import the task assets.
- Key out the green background from the foreground source clip.
- Build the composite yourself in Resolve so the result matches the intended target scene shown by the first-frame screenshot.
- Use the screenshot to determine which foreground subject to preserve and how the finished composite should look in placement, scale, and overall composition.
- Treat the screenshot as a visual specification for the background scene, framing, and composition that your final render should match.
- Do not export the untouched source clip and do not leave visible green-screen background in the final render.

Output:
- Export/render to `output/output.mp4`.

input_materials.name: Input target screenshot
input_materials.description: First-frame target screenshot showing which foreground subject should be preserved and what the intended finished composite should look like; not a packaged project and not an output asset

input_materials_summary: Input clip [`.mp4`] @ input/input.mp4: Foreground source clip | Input target screenshot [`.png`] @ input/input.png: First-frame target screenshot showing which foreground subject should be preserved and what the intended finished composite should look like; not a packaged project and not an output asset

title: Music Transcription
instruction:
Goal: Transcribe a recorded piece into musical notation, export both a PDF score and a MIDI file with correct instrument assignments. You may use any music notation software available on the system.

Read the task specification from `task_brief.json` in the variant's `input/` directory. It contains:
- `title`: the title of the piece
- `composer`: the composer of the piece
- `tempo_bpm`: the tempo of the song
- `instruments`: list of instruments to transcribe, each with name, clef, and GM program number

Input audio files are available in the input directory:
- `reference_song.mp3` — MP3 recording of the target song

## Steps
1. Listen to the reference audio to identify all instrumental parts.
2. Open a music notation software and create a new project.
3. Fill in the Title and Composer from `task_brief.json` in the project info.
4. Add players/instruments matching those listed in `task_brief.json`.
5. Configure the time signature, key signature, and tempo marking based on what you hear.
6. Transcribe all notes, rhythms, dynamics, articulations, and expression markings for each part.
7. Format the score layout professionally (proper spacing, alignment, readable note density).
8. Configure playback: assign each instrument to the correct General MIDI (GM) program number as specified in `task_brief.json`.
9. Export outputs:
   - Export a PDF of the full score. Save it as `output/transcription.pdf`.
   - Export MIDI: save as `output/transcription.mid`.
10. Take ONE final screenshot showing the notation software with the score visible, saved as `output/overview.png`.

## Output files
- `transcription.pdf`: Exported PDF of the complete score with all parts.
- `transcription.mid`: Exported MIDI file. Each track must have correct MIDI Program Change messages matching the GM program numbers in `task_brief.json`.
- `overview.png`: Screenshot of the notation software showing the transcribed score with instrument staves visible.

input_materials.name: Task specification
input_materials.description: Piece title, composer, tempo_bpm, and per-instrument name / clef / GM program number.

input_materials.name: Reference recording
input_materials.description: Audio recording of the target piece that the agent transcribes.

input_materials_summary: Task specification [`.json`] @ input/task_brief.json: Piece title, composer, tempo_bpm, and per-instrument name / clef / GM program number. | Reference recording [`.mp3`] @ input/reference_song.mp3: Audio recording of the target piece that the agent transcribes.
